name: Forming Plurals
summary: Portuguese usually makes plurals by adding -s, with special rules for some endings.
explanation:
Most Portuguese nouns add -s to form the plural: livro becomes livros, casa becomes casas. The article changes too: o becomes os, a becomes as.

Words ending in a consonant or stressed vowel follow special patterns. Nouns ending in -r or -z add -es: flor becomes flores, luz becomes luzes. Nouns ending in -m change to -ns: homem becomes homens, jardim becomes jardins.

The trickiest group ends in -ão, which can become -ões (coração to corações), -ães (pão to pães), or -ãos (mão to mãos). These must be learned individually.
commonMistakes: Just adding -s to words ending in -m. They change to -ns: jardins.
Forgetting that -l endings become -is: animal to animais.
Guessing the -ão plural; coração to corações but pão to pães.
Leaving the article singular. O livro becomes os livros.
Adding -es to words that only need -s after a vowel.
tip: The -ão plurals are the main irregular group. When you learn such a noun, learn its plural at the same time.
